2605.03762.title: OracleProto: A Reproducible Framework for Benchmarking LLM Native Forecasting via Knowledge Cutoff and Temporal Masking
2605.03762.paper_summary: Large language models are moving from static text generators toward real-world decision-support systems, where forecasting is a composite capability that links information gathering, evidence integration, situational judgment, and action-oriented decision making. This capability is in broad demand across finance, policy, industry, and scientific research, yet its evaluation remains difficult: live benchmarks evaluate forecasts before answers exist, making them the cleanest way to measure forecasting ability, but they expire once events resolve; retrospective benchmarks are reproducible, but they cannot reliably distinguish genuine forecasting from facts a model may have already learned during pretraining. Prompting models to "pretend not to know" cannot replace a genuine knowledge boundary. We propose OracleProto, a reproducible framework for evaluating LLM native forecasting capability. OracleProto reconstructs resolved events into time-bounded forecasting samples by combining model-cutoff-aligned sample admission, tool-level temporal masking, content-level leakage detection, discrete answer normalization, and hierarchical scoring. Instantiated on a FutureX-Past-derived dataset with six contemporary LLMs, OracleProto distinguishes forecasting quality, sampling stability, and cost efficiency under controlled information boundaries, while reducing residual leakage to the $1\%$ level, an order of magnitude below tool-only temporal filtering. OracleProto turns LLM forecasting from one-off evaluation into an auditable, reusable, and trainable dataset-level capability, providing a unified interface for fair cross-model comparison and a controlled signal source for downstream SFT and RL. Code and data are available at https://github.com/MaYiding/OracleProto and https://huggingface.co/datasets/MaYiding/OracleProto.
2605.03762.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型正從靜態文本生成器轉向現實世界的決策支持系統，其中預測是一種綜合能力，連結了信息收集、證據整合、情境判斷和以行動為導向的決策制定。這種能力在金融、政策、工業和科學研究中廣泛需求，但其評估仍然困難：實時基準在答案存在之前評估預測，使其成為測量預測能力的最乾淨方式，但一旦事件解決便失效；回顧性基準可重複，但無法可靠地區分真正的預測與模型在預訓練期間可能已經學習到的事實。促使模型「假裝不知道」無法替代真正的知識邊界。我們提出OracleProto，一個可重複的框架，用於評估LLM原生預測能力。OracleProto通過結合模型截止對齊的樣本接納、工具級時間屏蔽、內容級洩漏檢測、離散答案正規化和分層評分，將已解決的事件重建為時間限制的預測樣本。在一個基於FutureX-Past的數據集上，使用六個當代LLM實例化，OracleProto在受控信息邊界下區分預測質量、取樣穩定性和成本效率，同時將殘餘洩漏降低至$1\%$的水平，這比僅使用工具的時間過濾低一個量級。OracleProto將LLM預測從一次性評估轉變為可審計、可重用和可訓練的數據集級能力，提供了一個統一的接口以便進行公平的跨模型比較，以及一個受控的信號源以供下游SFT和RL使用。代碼和數據可在https://github.com/MaYiding/OracleProto和https://huggingface.co/datasets/MaYiding/OracleProto獲得。
2605.03762.author: Yiding Ma et.al.
2605.03762.authors: Yiding Ma, Chengyun Ruan, Kaibo Huang, Zhongliang Yang, Linna Zhou
